description_long: This is a large, two-story suburban house with a complex, multi-volume structure and a combination of brick and siding exterior finishes. The roof is predominantly dark gray and features multiple intersecting gable forms, giving the house a dynamic and layered appearance. The front facade includes a prominent entryway with a covered porch, supported by columns, and several large windows that provide ample natural light. The house has a mix of symmetrical and asymmetrical window arrangements, with some sections featuring bay windows and others with standard rectangular panes. The rear of the house includes a patio area and additional entrances, suggesting access to a backyard or garden. The property is set on a rectangular green lawn with a paved driveway and walkways, typical of residential neighborhoods.
description: A large, two-story suburban house with intersecting gable roofs, brick and siding exterior, and a prominent covered entryway. Features include multiple window styles, a patio, and a green lawn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, large windows, and a mix of brick and siding finishes.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended wing, secondary entrance, and a paved driveway area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the patio area, additional entrances, and a long stretch of windows along the back facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a combination of window styles, a secondary volume, and the transition between brick and siding materials.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of large and standard windows
attribute_entryway_design: Prominent covered porch with columns, wide steps, and a central door flanked by large windows
